title: Understanding the Problem
text: When the elevator moves upward at a constant velocity, what does this imply about the forces acting on the backpack?

title: Constant Velocity Implications
text: If the elevator is moving at a constant velocity, what can we say about the net force acting on the backpack?

title: Net Force and Weight
text: Recall that when an object moves at constant velocity, the net force acting on it is zero. How does this relate to the weight of the backpack?

title: Calculating the Weight
text: Use the formula for weight: $$weight=mass gravity$$. Given that the scale reads $$60$$ N when accelerating downward, calculate the actual weight of the backpack when the elevator moves at constant velocity.
